title: lobularia canariensis subsp. intermedia
intro: Lobularia canariensis subsp. intermedia, also known as sweet alyssum or low sweet alyssum, is a fragrant, annual herb in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) with delicate white or pale pink flowers. Lobularia canariensis subsp. intermedia has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and combat infections. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and supporting the immune system, due to its calming and sedative effects. Lobularia canariensis subsp. intermedia can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and its essential oil can be used in aromatherapy to promote well-being and calmness.
